pr.title: Match remaining Ness items except yoyo
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Matched a large batch of previously placeholder Ness item functions, covering PK Thunder ball/trail, PK Flash/Flush and explosion, PK Fire, and Ness bat, while explicitly leaving yoyo out of scope. The PR replaced many `/// #...` placeholders with C implementations, adjusted item-var structs and headers to support those implementations, and made a small Ness fighter-side stack/type correction for PK Thunder collision. decomp.dev reported 37 new matches, +9524 matched code bytes, and +200 matched data bytes.
what_changed: Implemented many Ness item functions that were previously `/// #...` placeholders, especially in `itnesspkthunderball.c`, `itnesspkflush.c`, `itnessbat.c`, `itnesspkthundertrail.c`, `itnesspkflushexplode.c`, and `itnesspkfire.c`.
Matched PK Thunder ball behavior including spawn/setup, animation, physics steering, angle normalization, trail position/angle history shifting, collision bounce handling, and related helper logic.
Matched PK Thunder trail behavior, adding `itNesspkthundertrail_ItemVars` with an owner/item reference and state fields in `itCharItems.h`.

Matched PK Flash/Flush behavior including item spawn, lifetime setup, owner linkage, charge/growth scale calculation, release/explosion transition, owner cleanup on destroy, held-input checks for Ness and Kirby-Ness variants, physics control, and collision handling.
Matched PK Flash explosion behavior including explosion item spawn/setup and animation-time scaling/ratio logic; changed `itPKFlushExplode_ItemVars.xDD8` from `s32` to `float`.

Matched Ness bat spawn and animation behavior, including item owner reference management, clearing fighter-side bat references through `ftNs_AttackS4_ItemNessBatSetNULL`, visibility flag toggling via JObj child flags, and copying scale from the owner when appropriate.
Matched PK Fire spawn and shield-bounce behavior, including setting the projectile rotation and negating JObj rotation Z when shield-bounced before calling `itColl_BounceOffShield`.

Simplified `itPKThunder_ItemVars` from many individually named `Vec3 xDEC`, `Vec3 xDFC`, etc. fields and angle scalar fields into `Vec3 positions[16]` and `f32 angles[16]`, plus corrected several later fields to `f32`/`s32` where the diff showed type changes.

Adjusted `ftNs_SpecialHi_ItemPKThunder_CheckNessCollide` stack-local modeling by changing collision helper arguments and locals from `Vec2` to `Vec3`, reducing adjacent explicit padding arrays accordingly.
Updated headers to expose corrected prototypes and item-var types, including `it_802AD478(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, Fighter_Part, f32)`.
smart_moves: Recognized repeated PK Thunder history fields as arrays: replacing a long sequence of offset-named `Vec3` and float fields with `positions[16]` and `angles[16]` made the implementation natural and supported loops for initialization, shifting, and reset.

Used small `static inline` helpers for repeated matching-sensitive logic, such as clearing Ness bat owner references, removing bat items, copying JObj scale, setting PK Flash scale, and shifting PK Thunder positions/angles.

Kept owner cleanup explicit across item/fighter boundaries: PK Flash destroy clears Ness or Kirby-Ness owner state depending on item kind, and Ness bat animation clears fighter-side bat references before nulling item owner fields.

Modeled spawn setup in full rather than relying on partial initialization: functions fill `SpawnItem` fields such as kind, prev/pos, facing direction, parent gobj fields, damage, velocity, and flags before `Item_80268B18`.
Preserved stack-sensitive matching details with `PAD_STACK(...)` and by correcting local vector widths in the Ness PK Thunder collision function.

Used article special attributes for behavior formulas instead of hardcoding derived values, for example PK Flash lifetime, graphic growth, hitbox growth, explosion delay, control, and PK Thunder speed/turn parameters.
Handled both Ness and Kirby-Ness PK Flash variants in the same matched logic by switching on item kind and calling the corresponding fighter hold/cleanup functions.
decomp_lessons: When many item-var fields at consecutive offsets have identical type and repeated use, try remodeling them as arrays; this can unlock loop-shaped source and reduce artificial offset-field clutter.
Item decomp often depends on correct fighter-item ownership semantics. Matched code may need both item-side owner/nulling and fighter-side callbacks to clear article references.

Struct field type corrections matter for matching: this PR changed examples such as `itNessbat_ItemVars.x0` from `s32` to `HSD_GObj*`, `itPKFlushExplode_ItemVars.xDD8` from `s32` to `float`, and PK Thunder trailing fields from `HSD_GObj*` guesses to `s32` where indicated.
Use `Vec3` rather than `Vec2` when the original stack layout includes z components, even if only x/y are compared; the `ftNs_SpecialHi` change shows this can alter nearby padding and matching.
For item spawn functions, matching often requires exact initialization order and explicit zeroing of velocity and itcmd vars.

For visual item behavior, JObj calls such as `HSD_JObjSetScale`, `HSD_JObjGetScaleY`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`, `HSD_JObjGetRotationZ`, and child flag operations can be central to matching animation callbacks.
The author noted that an item-decomp harness/skill plus Claude could now handle larger functions; this is evidence that guided tooling around item conventions was useful for this PR.
naming_conventions: Kept existing address-style function names where no semantic name was available, such as `it_802AB58C`, `it_802AA8C0`, `it_802AF940`, and `it_802AD478`.
Used item motion callback naming in the existing style: `itNesspkflush_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itNesspkthunderball_UnkMotion0_Phys`, `itNessbat_UnkMotion1_Anim`, etc.
Named new item-var structs with item-specific prefixes, e.g. `itNesspkthundertrail_ItemVars` and `itNessbat_ItemVars`.
Used descriptive array names `positions[16]` and `angles[16]` in `itPKThunder_ItemVars` instead of many offset-only names for repeated trail history state.
Retained offset-style placeholder member names where semantics remained unclear, e.g. `xDD4`, `xEEC`, `xEF0`, and PK Flash fields like `xDD8_PKFlash`.
Header prototype cleanup preferred project typedefs such as `Fighter_Part` and `f32` over generic `s32` and `float` where the diff showed stronger type evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` in multiple newly matched functions to preserve expected stack layout, including Ness bat spawn/anim helpers, PK Flash functions, PK Thunder animation/physics/collision helpers, and explosion functions.
Converted `ftNs_SpecialHi` collision locals from `Vec2` to `Vec3`, with corresponding padding changes, to match the stack shape implied by the original.
Used inline helpers to influence code generation while keeping repeated logic readable.
Normalized and wrapped angles explicitly in PK Thunder logic, using constants such as `M_PI`, `M_TAU`, and trigonometric calls `cosf`, `sinf`, and `atan2f` where shown in the diff.
Matched repeated history updates by shifting arrays backwards from index 14 to 0 and then writing the current position/angle at index 0.

Used decomp.me/auto-decomp evidence for at least one fighter-side function: the diff retained the comment `based on auto-decomp // https://decomp.me/scratch/hwphL // Proper match` near `ftNs_SpecialHi_ItemPKThunder_CheckNessCollide`.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided context.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 57.38% (+0.25%, +9524 bytes), matched data 37.65% (+0.02%, +200 bytes), and 37 new matches.
The bot's listed new matches included large functions in `itnesspkthunderball`, `itnesspkflush`, `itnesspkflushexplode`, `itnesspkthundertrail`, `itnesspkfire`, and `itnessbat`.
searchable_terms: Match remaining Ness items except yoyo
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match Ness yoyo after PR 2284.
Search for reuse of `itPKThunder_ItemVars.positions` and `angles` in later PK Thunder cleanup or renaming PRs.
Check whether later reviews renamed `it_802AB58C`, `it_802AA8C0`, `it_802AF940`, or other address-style Ness item functions.
Look for later `fighter-decomp` Skill PRs or comments by lukechampine following this item-decomp batch.
Compare other character item PRs for the same SpawnItem initialization and owner cleanup patterns.
